postData.contentHtml:
A powerful magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck the southern Philippines early Monday, centered at sea approximately 13 kilometers southwest of General Santos City. The tremor, originating from the Cotabato Trench at a depth of 10 kilometers, unleashed significant damage and prompted tsunami warnings for coastal areas in the Philippines, Indonesia, and Malaysia. Waves of up to 1 meter (3 feet) were observed in provinces like Sultan Kudarat and Sarangani.

President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. swiftly called for residents in vulnerable areas to evacuate to higher ground, emphasizing the paramount importance of life over possessions. He assured that the national government and disaster-response agencies were mobilized to assist Mindanao.

Initial reports indicated structural damage, including the partial collapse of a small commercial building in General Santos City, a major tuna-processing and commercial hub. Debris also struck parked tricycle taxis. While initial reports from one source mentioned no immediate casualties, subsequent news searches confirmed at least three fatalities. The quake struck during morning hours, disrupting school flag ceremonies and causing panic among schoolchildren, though no injuries were reported in these specific incidents.

The main shock was followed by aftershocks reaching magnitudes up to 6.5. The Philippines' susceptibility to such powerful seismic events is attributed to its location on the Pacific "Ring of Fire," an active arc of seismic faults and volcanoes.

Social media discussions surrounding the earthquake reflect active concern and widespread attention, underscoring the gravity of the event.
postData.title: Philippines Rocked by Powerful 7.8 Magnitude Earthquake, Tsunami Warnings Issued
postData.summary: A major undersea earthquake has struck the southern Philippines, causing significant damage, triggering tsunami alerts across the region, and resulting in casualties. President Marcos Jr. urged immediate evacuation to higher ground as aftershocks continue.

sources.url: A 7.8 magnitude earthquake rocks the southern Philippines - NPR
sources.title: A 7.8 magnitude earthquake rocks the southern Philippines - NPR

sources.url: Deadly 7.8 magnitude earthquake hits southern Philippines - CNN
sources.title: Deadly 7.8 magnitude earthquake hits southern Philippines - CNN
